file_summary: This Python module provides utilities for cloning GitHub repositories with error handling and authentication support. It includes a structured error class, a main function for cloning repositories with token authentication, and helper functions for error detection and message truncation.

line_windows.summary: This window covers the module imports, constant definitions, and the main clone_github_repository function up to the point where it handles git execution. It includes: 1) Module imports for dataclasses, os, pathlib, re, shutil, and subprocess; 2) GITHUB_URL_PATTERN regex for validating GitHub URLs; 3) RepoSourceError dataclass exception definition; 4) The clone_github_repository function definition with parameter validation, directory checks, git command preparation with authentication token support, and initial subprocess execution setup.

This window covers the error handling logic of clone_github_repository and all helper functions. It includes: 1) The completion of clone_github_repository function with git command execution error handling, cleanup on failure, and error categorization based on stderr/stdout analysis; 2) Three helper functions: _is_auth_error that detects authentication failures using keyword matching, _is_not_found_error that detects repository not found errors, and _truncate that limits error message length for cleaner output.
